name: Phase 4.6: Stripe Payment Flow
description: Add Stripe payment UI to the public-next registration flow: payment layout (Elements wrapper), payment form page, and post-redirect completion page. Also fix Stripe CLI Docker auth.

items.description: Install @stripe/stripe-js and @stripe/react-stripe-js in public-next. Fix Stripe CLI Docker container to use --api-key flag instead of interactive login.
items.planSection: Prerequisites + Docker Compose: Fix Stripe CLI Auth
tests: docker-compose.yml stripe service command includes --api-key flag
docker-compose.yml stripe service environment includes STRIPE_API_KEY
stepsToVerify: Run: pnpm --filter public-next add @stripe/stripe-js @stripe/react-stripe-js
Verify packages appear in apps/public-next/package.json
Update docker-compose.yml stripe service with --api-key ${STRIPE_API_KEY}
Run: docker compose up stripe -d — verify container starts without interactive login

items.description: Tracer bullet: hook fires onTimeout callback after specified duration when isProcessing is true.

tests.test: fires onTimeout after specified duration when processing
tests.behavior: Given isProcessing=true and timeoutDuration=5000, after 5000ms the onTimeout callback is called exactly once

stepsToVerify: RED: Write test, run pnpm --filter public-next test -- use-payment-timeout — test fails (module not found)
GREEN: Create lib/hooks/use-payment-timeout.ts (port from apps/public/src/hooks/use-payment-timeout.ts), run test — passes

items.description: Hook does NOT fire when isProcessing is false.

tests.test: does not fire onTimeout when not processing
tests.behavior: Given isProcessing=false, after any amount of time the onTimeout callback is never called

stepsToVerify: RED: Add test, run — should fail if implementation is wrong (or pass immediately if tracer bullet covered it)
GREEN: Verify existing implementation handles this — test passes

items.description: Hook clears timeout when isProcessing transitions from true to false.

tests.test: clears timeout when processing stops
tests.behavior: Start with isProcessing=true, rerender with isProcessing=false, advance timer past duration — onTimeout is never called

stepsToVerify: RED: Add test — should fail if cleanup is missing
GREEN: Verify implementation clears on isProcessing change — passes

items.description: Hook defaults to 120000ms (2 minutes) when no duration specified.

tests.test: uses default 120s timeout
tests.behavior: Given isProcessing=true with no timeoutDuration, onTimeout is NOT called at 119s but IS called at 120s

stepsToVerify: RED: Add test
GREEN: Verify default parameter value — passes

items.description: Tracer bullet: hook fetches stripe amount for a given payment ID and returns the data.

tests.test: fetches stripe amount for a given payment ID
tests.behavior: Given paymentId=42, the hook calls GET /api/payments/42/stripe-amount and returns { amountDue: { subtotal, transactionFee, total }, amountCents } on success

stepsToVerify: RED: Write test with mocked fetch and QueryClientProvider wrapper — fails (module not found)
GREEN: Create lib/hooks/use-stripe-amount.ts with useQuery fetching the endpoint — test passes

items.description: Hook is disabled (does not fetch) when paymentId is 0 or falsy.

tests.test: does not fetch when paymentId is 0
tests.behavior: Given paymentId=0, fetch is never called and the hook stays in idle/pending state

stepsToVerify: RED: Add test
GREEN: Ensure enabled: paymentId > 0 in useQuery options — passes

items.description: Hook returns error state when the API call fails.

tests.test: returns error state on fetch failure
tests.behavior: Given a 500 response from the API, the hook's isError is true

stepsToVerify: RED: Add test with mocked 500 response
GREEN: Ensure fetch function throws on non-ok response — passes

items.description: Tracer bullet: layout renders Stripe Elements provider with correct options when stripe amount loads successfully.

tests.test: renders Elements provider when stripe amount loads
tests.behavior: When useStripeAmount returns data with amountCents=2605, the layout renders an Elements provider wrapping its children. Children are visible in the DOM.

stepsToVerify: RED: Write test mocking useStripeAmount, @stripe/react-stripe-js (Elements), useRegistration, next/navigation — fails (module not found)
GREEN: Create [paymentId]/layout.tsx as 'use client' component with Elements wrapper and PaymentAmountContext — test passes

items.description: Layout calls initiateStripeSession() on mount to create a customer session for saved payment methods.

tests.test: calls initiateStripeSession on mount
tests.behavior: When the layout mounts, useRegistration().initiateStripeSession is called

stepsToVerify: RED: Add test with mock initiateStripeSession spy
GREEN: Add useEffect calling initiateStripeSession — passes

items.description: Layout shows nothing (returns null) while stripe amount is still loading.

tests.test: shows nothing while stripe amount is pending
tests.behavior: When useStripeAmount returns isPending=true, the Elements provider is NOT in the DOM and children are NOT rendered

stepsToVerify: RED: Add test with isPending mock
GREEN: Add early return null when isPending — passes

items.description: Layout provides stripe amount data to children via PaymentAmountContext (useCurrentPaymentAmount hook).

tests.test: provides amount data via useCurrentPaymentAmount
tests.behavior: A child component can call useCurrentPaymentAmount() and receive { amount: { subtotal, transactionFee, total } } matching the API response

stepsToVerify: RED: Add test with a child component that uses useCurrentPaymentAmount
GREEN: Wrap children in PaymentAmountContext.Provider with the fetched amount — passes

items.description: Tracer bullet: payment page renders the Stripe PaymentElement and a submit button.

tests.test: renders PaymentElement and submit button
tests.behavior: The page renders a PaymentElement (mocked as a test div) and a button with text 'Submit Payment'

stepsToVerify: RED: Write test mocking @stripe/react-stripe-js, registration context, auth context, useCurrentPaymentAmount, next/navigation — fails (module not found)
GREEN: Create [paymentId]/payment/page.tsx with PaymentElement and submit button — test passes

items.description: Payment page displays the formatted amount due from the payment layout context.

tests.test: displays the amount due
tests.behavior: When useCurrentPaymentAmount returns total=26.05, the page shows '$26.05' in the amount due text

stepsToVerify: RED: Add test checking for formatted amount text
GREEN: Add amount display using formatCurrency — passes

items.description: Successful submit flow: elements.submit() validates, createPaymentIntent() creates intent, stripe.confirmPayment() confirms with return_url pointing to ../complete.

tests.test: executes full submit flow on button click
tests.behavior: Click submit → elements.submit() called → createPaymentIntent() called → stripe.confirmPayment() called with clientSecret from intent and return_url containing '/complete'

stepsToVerify: RED: Add test with mock submit/intent/confirm chain
GREEN: Implement handleSubmit with three-phase flow — passes

items.description: When elements.submit() returns an error, the flow stops — createPaymentIntent is NOT called.

tests.test: stops flow when elements validation fails
tests.behavior: When elements.submit() resolves with { error: { message: 'Card declined' } }, createPaymentIntent is never called and the error message is displayed

stepsToVerify: RED: Add test with failing elements.submit mock
GREEN: Add early return after submit error — passes

items.description: Back button navigates to the review page and updates the registration step to ReviewStep.

tests.test: back button navigates to review
tests.behavior: Click back button → updateStep(ReviewStep) is called and router.replace is called with a path ending in '/review'

stepsToVerify: RED: Add test
GREEN: Add handleBack with updateStep and router.replace — passes

items.description: Submit button is disabled when Stripe is not loaded, elements not ready, payment is processing, or payment was already submitted.

tests.test: submit button disabled when stripe/elements not loaded
tests.behavior: When useStripe() returns null, the submit button has disabled attribute

stepsToVerify: RED: Add test with null stripe mock
GREEN: Add disabled condition on button — passes

items.description: Payment page adds window.onbeforeunload handler during payment processing to warn about leaving.

tests.test: adds beforeunload handler during processing
tests.behavior: When paymentProcessing is true, window has a beforeunload listener. When false, the listener is removed.

stepsToVerify: RED: Add test spying on window.addEventListener
GREEN: Add useEffect that manages beforeunload based on processing state — passes

items.description: Tracer bullet: complete page shows 'Payment Complete' title and formatted amount when payment intent status is 'succeeded'.

tests.test: shows success message when payment succeeded
tests.behavior: When stripe.retrievePaymentIntent resolves with status='succeeded', the page shows 'Payment Complete' heading and the formatted total amount

stepsToVerify: RED: Write test mocking useStripe (retrievePaymentIntent), useSearchParams (with client_secret), registration context, auth context, useCurrentPaymentAmount — fails (module not found)
GREEN: Create [paymentId]/complete/page.tsx with useEffect fetching intent status and conditional rendering — test passes

items.description: Complete page shows the user's email in the confirmation message (for email receipt notification).

tests.test: shows user email in confirmation on success
tests.behavior: When payment succeeded, the page shows text containing the authenticated user's email address

stepsToVerify: RED: Add test checking for user email in output
GREEN: Include user.email in the success message — passes

items.description: Complete page shows processing message when payment intent status is 'processing'.

tests.test: shows processing message
tests.behavior: When retrievePaymentIntent returns status='processing', the page shows text about the payment being processed by the bank

stepsToVerify: RED: Add test with processing status mock
GREEN: Add conditional branch for processing — passes

items.description: Complete page shows verification required message when payment intent status is 'requires_action'.

tests.test: shows verification required for requires_action
tests.behavior: When retrievePaymentIntent returns status='requires_action', the page shows 'Additional verification required' or similar

stepsToVerify: RED: Add test
GREEN: Add conditional branch — passes

items.description: Complete page shows failure message with a 'Try Again' link back to the payment page when status is 'requires_payment_method'.

tests.test: shows payment failed with retry link
tests.behavior: When retrievePaymentIntent returns status='requires_payment_method', the page shows 'Payment failed' text and a link pointing to '../payment'

stepsToVerify: RED: Add test checking for failure text and retry link href
GREEN: Add conditional branch with Link to ../payment — passes

items.description: Complete page shows error when retrievePaymentIntent itself returns an error.

tests.test: shows error when retrievePaymentIntent fails
tests.behavior: When stripe.retrievePaymentIntent resolves with { error: { message: 'Something went wrong' } }, the page shows 'Payment Failed' title and the error message

stepsToVerify: RED: Add test
GREEN: Handle error branch in useEffect — passes

items.description: Complete page shows error when payment_intent_client_secret is missing from URL search params.

tests.test: shows error when client secret is missing
tests.behavior: When useSearchParams returns no payment_intent_client_secret param, the page shows an error about the missing secret

stepsToVerify: RED: Add test with empty search params
GREEN: Add early check for missing clientSecret — passes

items.description: Complete page renders navigation links: 'See All Players' (to event registrations) and 'Home' (to /home).

tests.test: renders navigation links to registrations and home
tests.behavior: The page renders a link with text 'See All Players' pointing to the event's registrations URL, and a link with text 'Home' pointing to '/home'

stepsToVerify: RED: Add test checking for both links and their hrefs
GREEN: Add Link components in the footer — passes

items.description: All new and existing tests pass, typecheck succeeds, lint is clean, and build completes.
tests: pnpm --filter public-next test passes with 0 failures
pnpm --filter public-next typecheck passes with 0 errors
pnpm --filter public-next lint passes with 0 warnings or errors
stepsToVerify: Run: pnpm --filter public-next test
Run: pnpm --filter public-next typecheck
Run: pnpm --filter public-next lint
Run: pnpm --filter public-next build
